title: Court of Justice of the European Union (CJEU) Traineeship
description: A five-month paid traineeship at the Court of Justice of the European Union in Luxembourg, working either in the Court's services or in a Member's Chambers. Around 200 places are available each year. Trainees receive a monthly grant of roughly EUR 2,400 (75% of the minimum social wage for a skilled worker in Luxembourg) -- among the highest-paid traineeships of any EU institution. Trainees living 200km or more from Luxembourg receive a one-time travel contribution of EUR 150 toward their trip; this is modest and won't cover long-haul travel, so the monthly grant is the main source of support for living costs. Open to nationals of an EU Member State (with duly justified exceptions considered on a case-by-case basis) who hold a university degree in law, political science, economics, or a related field. This entry covers the March-July 2027 session: applications are open now, with a deadline of September 15, 2026.
org: Court of Justice of the European Union
visaNote: Primarily restricted to EU nationals, with limited exceptions considered case-by-case; non-EU citizens should confirm current eligibility before applying.
